message:
test(pattern_trigger): metamorphic oracles for scan output

TriggerScanner is the fast-path classifier for terminal output: it
decides whether pane bytes contain errors, completions, or progress
markers, and drives backpressure + alerting off the counts it returns.
A tokenizer regression or Aho-Corasick boundary drift silently changes
every downstream decision that depends on it.

Six relations, all structural (independent of which patterns are loaded):

- MR1 idempotence: scan_counts and scan_locate are pure — running twice
  on the same input must yield byte-identical results across all six
  TriggerCategory buckets.
- MR2 empty-input neutrality: scanning 0 bytes yields 0 matches,
  bytes_scanned=0, and an empty counts map, regardless of pattern set.
  Asserted both on the default scanner and a custom scanner.
- MR3 prefix-shift invariance: prepending a "safe" prefix (one that
  contains no triggers, verified inline via prop_assume) cannot remove
  any existing match. Every match survives with its offset shifted by
  prefix.len() and its pattern_index / category preserved. Additional
  boundary-straddling matches may appear but never fewer.
- MR4 concat subadditivity on counts: total_matches is monotone
  non-decreasing under concatenation. Joining two chunks may expose
  new boundary matches but cannot remove existing ones. bytes_scanned
  always equals input length.
- MR5 CI-only scanner is case-insensitive: a scanner built exclusively
  from case_insensitive patterns returns identical total_matches for
  lowercase / uppercase / mixed-case versions of the same text.
- MR6 locate bounds: every TriggerMatch has length > 0, offset+length
  <= input.len(), and pattern_index within scanner.pattern_count().
  A zero-length or out-of-bounds match is a decoder bug.

Plus a canary: `default_scanner_still_has_multi_category_coverage`
fails if the default pattern set drifts to a shape where some categories
become unreachable — otherwise MR1/MR3/MR4/MR6 become vacuously true on
those categories and silently stop detecting regressions.

Run: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test metamorphic_pattern_trigger_scan
9 passed / 0 failed in 1.64s with 200 proptest cases per relation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
